28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92

97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape

104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)

133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

import importlib.util

from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;

ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS - KEY FILES AND FUNCTIONS

**Primary File:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines, 105KB)

#### Core Save Functions:

| `unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` | `(self, repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)` | Instance method: pushes merged model to Hugging Face Hub |
| `patch_saving_functions()` | `(model, vision=False)` | Patches model with save/export methods (adds `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, etc.) |

#### GGUF/GGML Export Functions:

- `prepare_saving()` - Imported from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
- `convert_to_gguf()` - Imported from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp` as `_convert_to_gguf`

- `print_quantization_methods()` - Lists available quantization methods

### 3. LORA ADAPTER HANDLING - KEY FILES AND IMPLEMENTATIONS

**Primary Files:**
- `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (451 lines)
- `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2266-2900, key sections)
- `/unsloth/models/loader.py`
- `/unsloth/models/_utils.py` (68KB)

#### LoRA Architecture Classes (in `fast_lora.py`):

```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Implements: G = G + Ag @ Bg, U = U + Au @ Bu, W = W + Aw @ Bw
    # Used for SwiGLU/MLP layers with gate, up, down projections
    # Tracks: gateA, gateB, gateS, upA, upB, upS, downA, downB, downS

class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Implements: Q = Q + QA @ QB, K = K + KA @ KB, V = V + VA @ VB
    # Used for attention QKV projections
    # Tracks: QA, QB, QS, KA, KB, KS, VA, VB, VS

class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Implements: W = W + A @ B (generic matrix multiplication)
    # Used for output projection and other generic layers
    # Tracks: A (down), B (up), S (scaling factor)
```

#### LoRA Configuration (in `llama.py`, `get_peft_model()` method):

**Key Parameters:**
- `r` (rank): default 16 - LoRA decomposition rank
- `lora_alpha`: default 16 - LoRA scaling factor
- `lora_dropout`: default 0.0 - Dropout during training
- `target_modules`: Default list includes `["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj", "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]`
- `max_lora_rank`: default 16
- `use_rslora`: Whether to use Rank-Stabilized LoRA
- `modules_to_save`: List of modules to save without LoRA (e.g., embed_tokens, lm_head)
- `loftq_config`: Config for Low-Rank-Finetune-without-Quantization initialization
- `init_lora_weights`: Whether to initialize LoRA weights

#### LoRA Parameters Access (in `kernels/utils.py`):

Functions imported from `kernels/__init__.py`:
- `get_lora_parameters()` - Extracts LoRA A/B matrices
- `get_lora_parameters_bias()` - Extracts LoRA biases if enabled
- `matmul_lora()` - Performs W = W + (A @ B) multiplication
- `fast_dequantize()` - Dequantizes 4-bit weights to float32

#### LoRA Merging:

- Uses `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils`
- Handles mixed quantization states (4-bit + LoRA)
- Outputs: float16 merged weights or 4-bit merged weights

---

### 4. CODEBASE ORGANIZATION - MAIN ENTRY POINTS

#### Primary Entry Points:

1. **Model Loading/Fine-tuning:**
   - `from unsloth import FastLanguageModel` (in `/models/loader.py`)
   - `from unsloth import FastVisionModel` (for vision models)
   - `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` - Loads model with LoRA patches
   - `FastLanguageModel.get_peft_model()` - Adds LoRA adapters

2. **Model Saving:**
   - `model.save_pretrained()` - Standard HF format
   - `model.save_pretrained_merged()` - Saves merged weights
   - `model.save_pretrained_gguf()` - GGUF format (llama.cpp compatible)
   - `model.save_pretrained_ggml()` - GGML format (LoRA only)
   - `model.save_pretrained_torchao()` - TorchAO quantized format
   - `model.push_to_hub_merged()` - Pushes merged to HF Hub
   - `model.push_to_hub_gguf()` - Pushes GGUF to HF Hub

3. **Training:**
   - `from unsloth.trainer import patch_trl_trainer` - Patches TRL trainers for speed
   - Uses standard `transformers` + `peft` + `trl` training loops

4. **Utilities:**
   - `from unsloth import is_bfloat16_supported`
   - `from unsloth import is_vLLM_available`
   - Chat templates via `chat_templates.py`

#### Module Dependencies:
- **Required:** `torch`, `transformers`, `peft`, `bitsandbytes`, `triton`
- **Optional:** `trl`, `xformers`, `unsloth_zoo` (external dependency)
- **External:** `unsloth_zoo` - Contains `merge_and_overwrite_lora()`, GGUF/GGML conversion, llama.cpp integration

---

### 5. EXISTING ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Finding:** Limited ONNX support in this codebase. The search revealed only 1 reference to "ort_" (unrelated):
- `/unsloth/models/loader.py` line 874: `import_from_cache = False` (unrelated variable)

**Current Export Formats Supported:**
1. **Hugging Face Format** (default) - Uses `save_pretrained()`
2. **GGUF Format** - llama.cpp compatible quantization via llama.cpp
3. **GGML Format** - LoRA-only export for llama.cpp
4. **TorchAO Format** - PyTorch quantization via torchao library

**No Native ONNX Export:** The codebase does not currently have native ONNX export functionality. ONNX conversion would need to be added as a new feature.

---

#### Kernel Classes (in `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):

```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X: torch.Tensor,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
                upW, upW_quant, upA, upB, upS,
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
                _forward_function, _backward_function,
                inplace=True) -> torch.Tensor

    @staticmethod
    def backward(ctx, dY: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]

class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Similar structure for QKV projections

class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Similar structure for generic weights
```

---

### 7. SAVE/EXPORT WORKFLOW

**Flow for LoRA Saving:**
1. User calls `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_16bit")`
2. `patch_saving_functions()` routes to `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`
3. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` which delegates to `unsloth_save_model()`
4. `unsloth_save_model()` extracts LoRA A/B matrices from peft config
5. Calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo` to merge with base weights
6. Saves merged weights using `model.save_pretrained()` with appropriate dtype

**Flow for GGUF Saving:**
1. User calls `model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method="fast_quantized")`
2. Routes to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`
3. First merges LoRA to float16 via `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
4. Installs llama.cpp if needed
5. Calls `convert_to_gguf()` from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
6. Optionally quantizes via llama-quantize tool
7. Returns path to `.gguf` file

---

### 8. TEST COVERAGE

Test files in `/tests/saving/`:
- `test_unsloth_save.py` - General save functionality
- `language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_mistral.py`, `test_merge_4bit_validation.py`, etc.
- `language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py`, `test_push_to_hub_merged_sharded_index_file.py`
- `language_models/test_save_merged_grpo_model.py`
- Vision and text-to-speech specific save tests

---

### SUMMARY

Unsloth is a highly optimized fine-tuning framework that:
1. **Focuses on speed and memory efficiency** through CUDA kernels for LoRA
2. **Supports multiple save formats**: HF standard, merged weights (16bit/4bit), GGUF, GGML, TorchAO
3. **Uses external `unsloth_zoo` for heavy lifting**: LoRA merging, GGUF conversion
4. **Has minimal ONNX support**: No native ONNX export exists
5. **Integrates deeply with transformers/PEFT ecosystem**: Patches saving functions at runtime
6. **Supports 10+ model architectures**: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, Granite, Cohere, etc.
7. **Key innovation**: Fast LoRA kernels (`LoRA_MLP`, `LoRA_QKV`, `LoRA_W`) with custom autograd

   - `from unsloth.trainer import patch_trl_trainer` - Patches TRL trainers for speed
   - Uses standard `transformers` + `peft` + `trl` training loops

4. **Utilities:**
   - `from unsloth import is_bfloat16_supported`
   - `from unsloth import is_vLLM_available`
   - Chat templates via `chat_templates.py`

#### Module Dependencies:
- **Required:** `torch`, `transformers`, `peft`, `bitsandbytes`, `triton`
- **Optional:** `trl`, `xformers`, `unsloth_zoo` (external dependency)
- **External:** `unsloth_zoo` - Contains `merge_and_overwrite_lora()`, GGUF/GGML conversion, llama.cpp integration

---

### 5. EXISTING ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Finding:** Limited ONNX support in this codebase. The search revealed only 1 reference to "ort_" (unrelated):
- `/unsloth/models/loader.py` line 874: `import_from_cache = False` (unrelated variable)

**Current Export Formats Supported:**
1. **Hugging Face Format** (default) - Uses `save_pretrained()`
2. **GGUF Format** - llama.cpp compatible quantization via llama.cpp
3. **GGML Format** - LoRA-only export for llama.cpp
4. **TorchAO Format** - PyTorch quantization via torchao library

**No Native ONNX Export:** The codebase does not currently have native ONNX export functionality. ONNX conversion would need to be added as a new feature.

---

### 6. KEY CLASS NAMES AND METHOD SIGNATURES

#### Model Classes:

```python
# In /unsloth/models/loader.py
class FastLanguageModel:
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(model_name, max_seq_length, dtype, load_in_4bit, ...)
    @staticmethod
    def get_peft_model(model, r=16, lora_alpha=16, target_modules=[...], ...)

class FastVisionModel:
    @staticmethod
    def from_pretrained(model_name, max_seq_length, dtype, load_in_4bit, ...)
    @staticmethod
    def get_peft_model(model, r=16, target_modules=None, ...)

class FastModel:
    # Generic model class
```

#### Model-Specific Classes:

```python
# In respective files
class FastLlamaModel:        # /unsloth/models/llama.py
class FastMistralModel:      # /unsloth/models/mistral.py
class FastQwen2Model:        # /unsloth/models/qwen2.py
class FastQwen3Model:        # /unsloth/models/qwen3.py
class FastGemmaModel:        # /unsloth/models/gemma.py
class FastGraniteModel:      # /unsloth/models/granite.py
class FastFalconH1Model:     # /unsloth/models/falcon_h1.py
```

#### Main Save API (attached via `patch_saving_functions()`):

```python
# Instance methods added to any model
model.save_pretrained(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    save_method: str = "lora",  # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process: bool = True,
    state_dict: Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function: Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size: Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization: bool = True,
    variant: Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format: bool = True,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.9,
)

model.save_pretrained_merged(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",  # ["16bit", "4bit", "lora"]
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    # ... other params
)

model.save_pretrained_gguf(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion: str = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    # Supports: "not_quantized", "fast_quantized", "quantized", "f32", "f16",
    #          "q8_0", "q4_k_m", "q5_k_m", "q2_k", "q3_k_*", "q4_*", "q5_*", "q6_k", "iq2_*", "iq3_*"
)

model.push_to_hub_merged(
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer = None,
    save_method: str = "merged_16bit",
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    # ... other params
)

model.push_to_hub_gguf(
    repo_id: str,
    tokenizer = None,
    quantization_method: str = "fast_quantized",
    # ... other params
)

model.save_pretrained_torchao(
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer = None,
    torchao_config = None,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,

```

#### Kernel Classes (in `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`):

```python
class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    @staticmethod
    def forward(ctx, X: torch.Tensor,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
                upW, upW_quant, upA, upB, upS,
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
                _forward_function, _backward_function,
                inplace=True) -> torch.Tensor

    @staticmethod
    def backward(ctx, dY: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, ...]

class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Similar structure for QKV projections

class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
    # Similar structure for generic weights
```

---

### 7. SAVE/EXPORT WORKFLOW

**Flow for LoRA Saving:**
1. User calls `model.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, save_method="merged_16bit")`
2. `patch_saving_functions()` routes to `unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()`
3. Calls `unsloth_generic_save()` which delegates to `unsloth_save_model()`
4. `unsloth_save_model()` extracts LoRA A/B matrices from peft config
5. Calls `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo` to merge with base weights
6. Saves merged weights using `model.save_pretrained()` with appropriate dtype

**Flow for GGUF Saving:**
1. User calls `model.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method="fast_quantized")`
2. Routes to `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()`
3. First merges LoRA to float16 via `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
4. Installs llama.cpp if needed
5. Calls `convert_to_gguf()` from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`
6. Optionally quantizes via llama-quantize tool
7. Returns path to `.gguf` file

---

### 8. TEST COVERAGE

Test files in `/tests/saving/`:
- `test_unsloth_save.py` - General save functionality
- `language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_mistral.py`, `test_merge_4bit_validation.py`, etc.
- `language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py`, `test_push_to_hub_merged_sharded_index_file.py`
- `language_models/test_save_merged_grpo_model.py`
- Vision and text-to-speech specific save tests

---

### SUMMARY

Unsloth is a highly optimized fine-tuning framework that:
1. **Focuses on speed and memory efficiency** through CUDA kernels for LoRA
2. **Supports multiple save formats**: HF standard, merged weights (16bit/4bit), GGUF, GGML, TorchAO
3. **Uses external `unsloth_zoo` for heavy lifting**: LoRA merging, GGUF conversion
4. **Has minimal ONNX support**: No native ONNX export exists
5. **Integrates deeply with transformers/PEFT ecosystem**: Patches saving functions at runtime
6. **Supports 10+ model architectures**: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, Granite, Cohere, etc.
7. **Key innovation**: Fast LoRA kernels (`LoRA_MLP`, `LoRA_QKV`, `LoRA_W`) with custom autograd

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model

def unsloth_save_model(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None: token = get_token()

    if commit_message is None: commit_message = ""
    if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
        commit_message += " (Trained with Unsloth)"
    commit_message = commit_message.lstrip()

    if commit_description is None:
        commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
    elif "Unsloth 2x faster" not in commit_description:
        commit_description += " (Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"
    pass

    save_pretrained_settings = dict(locals())
    for deletion in ("model", "tokenizer", "save_method", "temporary_location", "maximum_memory_usage"):
        del save_pretrained_settings[deletion]
    pass

    # First check for a token!
    if push_to_hub:
        from huggingface_hub import whoami
        try:
            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]
        except:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens"
            )
        pass
    pass

    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)

    # Clean memory up first
    for _ in range(3):
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
        gc.collect()
    pass

    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")
    if save_method != "lora" and save_method != "merged_16bit" and save_method != "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\
            '"lora"         ==> This is the fastest and easiet. Just saves LoRA modules.\n'\
            '"merged_16bit" ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to float16. Needed for llama.cpp / GGUF.\n'\
            '"merged_4bit"  ==> This merges LoRA weights and saves to 4bit. Useful for DPO / inference.'
        )
    pass

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":

        print("Unsloth: Merging 4bit and LoRA weights to 4bit...")
        print("This might take 5 minutes...")

        # Counteract no LoRA adapters!
        if hasattr(model, "merge_and_unload"):
            model = model.merge_and_unload()
        pass
        print("Done.")
    pass

    if tags is not None:
        assert(isinstance(tags, (list, tuple)))
        tags = list(tags) + ["unsloth",]
    else:
        tags = ["unsloth",]
    pass
    save_pretrained_settings["tags"] = tags

    if ((save_method == "lora") or (save_method == "merged_4bit")) and push_to_hub:
        if token is None:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "Unsloth: Pushing to HF requires a token. Pass `token = 'hf_....'`\n"\
                "Go to https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens."
            )
        pass

        if save_method == "lora":
            print("Unsloth: Saving LoRA adapters. Please wait...")
        elif save_method == "merged_4bit":
            print("Unsloth: Saving 4bit Bitsandbytes model. Please wait...")

30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu

37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)

47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)

51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY

57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)

72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,

import torch
from .utils import (
    _maybe_fake_quantize_activations,
    fast_dequantize,
    QUANT_STATE,
    get_lora_parameters,
    get_lora_parameters_bias,
    matmul_lora,
    torch_amp_custom_fwd,
    torch_amp_custom_bwd,
)


class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
    """
    ### LoRA weights
    G = G + Ag @ Bg
    U = U + Au @ Bu
    W = W + Aw @ Bw

    ### SwiGLU(X)
    e = X @ G
    f = e * sigmoid(e)
    g = X @ U
    h = f * g
    i = h @ W

    ### Backpropagation chain rule
    See our blog post for more details

    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)

    ### Down projection LoRA weights
    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY

    ### Up projection LoRA weights
    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)

    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)

    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
    """
    @staticmethod
    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
                _forward_function, _backward_function,
                inplace = True,):
        dtype = X.dtype

        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
        h = _forward_function(e, g)
        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)

def unsloth_generic_save(
    model,
    tokenizer,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike] = "unsloth_finetuned_merge",
    save_method          : str = "lora", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,

    # Push to hub
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : List[str] = None,

    # Our functions
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.9,
):
    if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()

    if save_method == "merged_4bit":
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Unsloth: Merging into 4bit will cause your model to lose accuracy if you plan\n"\
            "to merge to GGUF or others later on. I suggest you to do this as a final step\n"\
            "if you're planning to do multiple saves.\n"\
            "If you are certain, change `save_method` to `merged_4bit_forced`."
        )
    elif save_method == "merged_4bit_forced":
        save_method = "merged_4bit"

    merge_and_overwrite_lora(
        get_model_name,
        model                = model,
        tokenizer            = tokenizer,
        save_directory       = save_directory,
        push_to_hub          = push_to_hub,
        private              = private,
        token                = token,
        save_method          = save_method,
        output_dtype         = None,
        low_disk_space_usage = True,
        use_temp_file        = False,
    )
    return
pass


def unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    is_main_process      : bool = True,
    state_dict           : Optional[dict] = None,
    save_function        : Callable = torch.save,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str] = "5GB",
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    variant              : Optional[str] = None,
    save_peft_format     : bool = True,
    tags                 : List[str] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass


def unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged(
    self,
    repo_id              : str,
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]
    use_temp_dir         : Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message       : Optional[str] = "Trained with Unsloth",
    private              : Optional[bool] = None,
    token                : Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    max_shard_size       : Union[int, str, None] = "5GB",
    create_pr            : bool = False,
    safe_serialization   : bool = True,
    revision             : str = None,
    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.push_to_hub(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]          = self
    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = True
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["repo_id"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

Now I have all the information I need. Let me now understand the PEFT model structure for accessing LoRA A/B weights. Based on what I know about PEFT:

1. `model.peft_config` is a dict where keys are adapter names (e.g., "default")
2. Each config has: `.r` (rank), `.lora_alpha`, `.target_modules` (list), `.lora_dropout`, `.bias`
3. LoRA layers are accessible via `model.named_modules()` and have `.lora_A` and `.lora_B` attributes (OrderedDicts where keys are adapter names)


4. The actual weight tensors are at `.lora_A[adapter_name].weight` and `.lora_B[adapter_name].weight`

So my implementation plan is to create a new function that takes the model and save directory, then pulls the rank and alpha from the config, iterates through all named modules to find the LoRA layers, and extracts their weight matrices for each adapter.

For the ONNX export, I need to think about the math: each LoRA layer applies `(x @ lora_A.T @ lora_B.T) * (alpha / r)` to the base output, where A has shape `[r, in_features]` and B has shape `[out_features, r]`. I'll export each A/B pair as separate ONNX files with the scaling factor included in the metadata. A, B), we could create a simple ONNX graph that computes the LoRA adaptation: `delta = (x @ A.T) @ B.T * scale`
- Or we could just save the weights as ONNX tensors (constants)

The most useful approach for ONNX would be to export a "LoRA adaptation" ONNX model per layer that takes an input tensor and computes the LoRA delta. But this is complex because the input shape varies.

A simpler and more practical approach:
- Save each LoRA adapter (per named module) as an ONNX graph that encodes the A and B matrices as initializers (constants)
- The graph takes an input tensor `x` of shape `[batch, seq, in_features]` and outputs the LoRA delta `[batch, seq, out_features]`
- Scale = lora_alpha / r is baked in

Now I'm weighing the practical options: I could create one ONNX file per LoRA layer with the A and B matrices as a mini-graph, but that means many files. Alternatively, I could bundle everything into a single file with all LoRA layers as named tensors, though ONNX doesn't have a pure tensor store format. Or I could have one file per target module containing the delta computation as a subgraph. I'm settling on a cleaner approach: create one ONNX file per adapter that contains all the A and B matrices as constant tensors, with the LoRA computation encoded as a sequence of matrix multiplication operations. Writing the export function... I'm checking that the PEFT config exists, then extracting rank, alpha, and target_modules from each adapter and saving that metadata to a JSON file. Now I'm collecting the LoRA weight matrices from the model. Clarifying the weight dimensions and how they map through the linear layers...

Now I'm verifying that my ONNX initializers are set up correctly with the transposed weights, and I need to check how the scaling factor is applied in PEFT's implementation.

For the LoRA scaling, I'll use the standard `alpha / r` formula as the default and document that in the docstring. I also need to make sure the ONNX IR version is set properly for opset 17, which should be version 8.

Looking at where to integrate this into the codebase, I'll add the new ONNX export function before the existing `patch_saving_functions`, then hook it into the model's methods in both the vision and non-vision code paths, and optionally update the `__all__` exports.

Writing the ONNX export function... Now I'm checking that the PEFT configuration exists on the model, creating the save directory, and then iterating through each adapter to extract its metadata like rank, alpha, and target modules into a dictionary. Extracting the LoRA weight matrices from both linear layers and converting them to NumPy arrays, then determining the input/output feature dimensions and rank from the shapes, before pulling the PEFT configuration for this specific adapter to resolve the rank and alpha scaling parameters. Building the ONNX graph... Finishing up the metadata for the exported LoRA adapter by sanitizing the module name for the filename, saving the ONNX model to disk, and storing the layer information including rank, alpha, and scale parameters in the export registry.

Now I'm checking if any layers were actually exported, and if not, logging a warning that no LoRA weight pairs were found. Then I'm consolidating all the metadata—opset version, adapter info, and exported layers—into a single JSON file that gets written to the save directory.

After that, I'm printing a summary message showing how many LoRA layers were exported and to where, then returning the save directory path. Finally, I need to integrate this new export function into the patching system by attaching it as a method to the model in both the vision and non-vision code paths, and make sure it's exported in the `__all__` list.

Now I'm deciding on the method name—the user specifically requested `export_to_onnx`, but looking at the existing naming pattern with `save_pretrained_merged`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, and similar methods, I should probably follow that convention. # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model

# llama.cpp specific targets - all takes 90s. Below takes 60s
LLAMA_CPP_TARGETS = ["llama-quantize", "llama-export-lora", "llama-cli",]

# Check environments
keynames = "\n" + "\n".join(os.environ.keys())

# Weights

)
LLAMA_LAYERNORMS = (

# https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/blob/master/examples/quantize/quantize.cpp#L19
# From https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/Quantize_Llama_2_models_using_ggml.html
ALLOWED_QUANTS = \
{
    "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
    "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",

    "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",

    "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
    "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
    "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
    "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
    "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
    "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
    "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
    "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
    "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
    "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
